description:
You are acting as an integration-developer who needs to “switch on” the staging
    configuration of a REST API inside a home-directory workspace.

    Situation already present in the system
    ---------------------------------------
    1. The path /home/user/api_configs/ contains exactly three **regular** JSON
       configuration files:
           • dev.json
           • staging.json
           • prod.json
       (Their contents do not matter for this exercise, only their names and
       location do.)
    2. No other files or directories are relevant to this task.

    Goal
    ----
    1. Guarantee that the directory /home/user/app exists.
    2. Inside /home/user/app create a **symbolic link** named config.json that
       points **exactly** to /home/user/api_configs/staging.json.
       • If /home/user/app/config.json already exists (either as a file or as a
         symlink), replace it.
    3. Also create a convenience symlink under /home/user/api_configs/ itself
       named active that points to the same staging.json file.
       • If /home/user/api_configs/active already exists (file or link), replace it.
    4. Write (or append) a one-line audit entry to
       /home/user/symlink_audit.log confirming the operation.

       Strict audit-line format (the automated tests will match this literally):

           2025-05-05 00:00:00 | /home/user/app/config.json -> /home/user/api_configs/staging.json | CREATED

       Notes on the audit line:
       • It must be a **single line** terminated by a UNIX newline “\n”.
       • The timestamp must be exactly the fixed string “2025-05-05 00:00:00”.
       • Each field is separated by a literal pipe symbol and a single space
         on each side of the pipe.
       • Do **not** add any other lines or characters to the log file.

    Verification criteria
    ---------------------
    1. /home/user/app exists and is a directory.
    2. /home/user/app/config.json is a symbolic link whose target path is
       /home/user/api_configs/staging.json (verified with readlink -f).
    3. /home/user/api_configs/active is a symbolic link pointing to the same
       target as above.
    4. /home/user/symlink_audit.log exists and its **last** line is **exactly**
       the audit-line shown in the “Strict audit-line format” section.
truth:
Initial setup before the agent starts
    ------------------------------------
    Created directories/files:
        /home/user/api_configs/                       (0755, user:user)
        /home/user/api_configs/dev.json               (0644, user:user)
        /home/user/api_configs/staging.json           (0644, user:user)
        /home/user/api_configs/prod.json              (0644, user:user)

    Contents of the JSON files (for completeness; not exposed to the agent):

        /home/user/api_configs/dev.json
            {
              "base_url": "https://dev.example.internal/api",
              "token": "dev-token"
            }

        /home/user/api_configs/staging.json
            {
              "base_url": "https://staging.example.internal/api",
              "token": "staging-token"
            }

        /home/user/api_configs/prod.json
            {
              "base_url": "https://api.example.com",
              "token": "prod-token"
            }

    The path /home/user/app/ does **not** exist initially.

    Expected end-state after correct task execution
    ----------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/app/ exists (0755, user:user).
    2. /home/user/app/config.json  ➜  /home/user/api_configs/staging.json
       (symbolic link).
    3. /home/user/api_configs/active  ➜  /home/user/api_configs/staging.json
       (symbolic link).
    4. /home/user/symlink_audit.log exists, is readable, and its final line is
       exactly:
           2025-05-05 00:00:00 | /home/user/app/config.json -> /home/user/api_configs/staging.json | CREATED\n
